semantic_journey: Arabic al-dhart = farting, offensive bodily emission. Greek loidora = insults, verbal abuse. Both offensive but from different organs and different domains: bodily gas vs verbal abuse. Crosses domain boundary. Too speculative.
key_insight: Arabic flatulence (bodily emission) and Greek verbal insults are entirely different domains; the offensive-emission metaphor is too weak.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-fawgh = mouth-related defect or openness. Greek polyphagos = gluttonous (polu+phagein). Mouth-defect and excessive-eating are both mouth-related but categorically different: structural defect vs behavioral excess.
key_insight: Arabic mouth-openness/defect and Greek excessive-eating are both mouth-related but in different categories (structural vs behavioral).

semantic_journey: Arabic al-shamaz = to speak with gentle words to soothe someone, gentle movement without violence. Greek hilasma = propitiation, atonement (from hilaskomai = to appease). Core: gentle verbal appeasement. 1-step drift: gentle-soothing-speech -> formal-appeasement-act.
key_insight: Arabic gentle-soothing speech and Greek propitiation/appeasement share words/acts aimed at calming and restoring favor.

semantic_journey: Arabic danu = a worthless fragment, cast-off, negligible. Greek -idion = a Greek diminutive grammatical suffix. A content word meaning worthless-fragment and a grammatical morpheme are fundamentally different categories and cannot form a cognate.
key_insight: A content word meaning worthless-fragment and a grammatical diminutive suffix are fundamentally different categories.

semantic_journey: Arabic radasa = to drop a stone to gauge depth. Greek rizais = dative plural of rhiza = tree roots. Depth-plumbing-stone and tree-roots require 3+ steps: depth-plumb -> bottom -> below-surface -> root. Different domains.
key_insight: Arabic depth-plumbing stone and Greek plant roots require 3+ steps to connect across different domains.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-itawa = tribute, tax imposed on subjects (al-kharaj). Greek Heilotes = Helots, Spartan serfs who paid forced dues. Core: forced extraction of wealth from subordinates. 1-step material-culture: tribute-system -> the-tribute-paying-class.
key_insight: Arabic al-itawa (tribute/tax) and Greek Helots (serfs paying forced dues) share forced economic extraction from subordinates.

semantic_journey: Arabic sataha = to have intercourse with (erotic domain). Greek stethea = chests, breasts (upper torso anatomy). Both body-related but different anatomical and functional domains. Getting from intercourse to chest-area requires 3+ steps.
key_insight: Arabic intercourse and Greek chest/breasts are both body-related but in different anatomical domains requiring 3+ steps.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-qazw = to play with something, poke at the ground. Greek laikaze = to perform the sexual act (vulgar). Playing-with/poking and a specific sexual act verb require 3 steps minimum: playful-poking -> erotic-play -> specific-sexual-act.
key_insight: Arabic playful-poking and Greek specific-sexual-act are in different categories; the erotic-play bridge requires 3+ steps.
